meaning: adj.全面的,广泛的,能充分理解的,包容的
examples: Streaming within comprehensive schools is common practice.
The idea was to create a comprehensive road map of the Web.
The Rough Guide to Nepal is a comprehensive guide to the region.
The Forbes Crash Course In Investing offers the most up-to-date, comprehensive guide to investing in today's markets.
Or eliminating 153 out of 154 separate health care programs when one good comprehensive one will do.
